title: Issue 2162 bounded pre-study before planning-shape commitment
lane_outcome: Implement bounded, evidence-triggered pre-study before Planning shape commitment, measure its activation cost, and reuse its disposable decision state for Planning creation and mixed-agent continuation.
purpose_for_parent: Satisfy #2162 and its child slices #2163 and #2164 without creating a parallel task authority or charging clear bounded work for unnecessary discovery.
subsystems: external intent and provider-agnostic relationship evidence
workspace CLI tests and shipped skills
technical_strategy: Introduce a compact provider-agnostic work-shape study packet and classifier that distinguishes Planning custody from known artefact shape. Route missing shape-changing evidence to bounded read-only probes, compile sufficient study state into the selected Planning owner, expose freshness/consumption semantics through existing continuation packets, and add comparative fixtures and operating-loop guidance for skip/activate/stop decisions.
technology_choices: existing Python dict packet builders and compact selectors

slice_sequence.title: #2162 bounded pre-study runtime and planning-shape route
slice_sequence.purpose_for_lane: Separate custody from shape, add bounded study/skip/ambiguity states, and route lane evidence before shape-specific mutation.

slice_sequence.title: #2163 comparative activation and completion-cost evidence
slice_sequence.purpose_for_lane: Demonstrate clear-task skip, shape-changing study, and apparent-uncertainty no-broadening cases with separate study cost and downstream savings.

slice_sequence.title: #2164 disposable study continuation and Planning seed state
slice_sequence.purpose_for_lane: Compile useful study evidence into canonical Planning and support mixed-agent continuation without rereading or durable duplicate authority.

current_slice: #2162 runtime foundation followed by #2163 and #2164 acceptance follow-through
acceptance_boundary: The lane is complete only when #2162, #2163, and #2164 acceptance criteria are implemented and demonstrated, including zero-study, unknown-to-lane, ambiguous/escalation, stale binding, Planning consumption, mixed-agent continuation, and comparative cost evidence.
proof_strategy: Use changed-path proof selection, focused startup/next-safe-action/external-intent/Planning/continuation tests, #2143 unknown-to-lane replay, three-class comparative dogfooding evidence, compact-output checks, full workspace test and lint lanes, and closeout intent/dogfooding review.

evidence: make test-workspace; make lint-workspace; make typecheck; 330 focused CLI tests; 179 workspace CLI tests; #2143 unknown-to-lane replay; comparative cost report; planning/runtime mirror checks
known_gaps: Command-owned lane activation should attach the active execplan automatically; tracked in #2165.

residual_lane_work: Command-owned lane activation should attach the active execplan automatically; tracked in #2165.
lane_to_epic_contribution: Completes #2162 and child slices #2163/#2164 with runtime, continuation, cost-evidence, guidance, and proof coverage.

notes: The child issues explicitly name both #2162 and #1969; this lane owns their pre-study outcome while reusing existing state-delta and continuation concepts from #1969 rather than duplicating them.
